babyLearning.en: growing little expressions, tiny brows and lips taking shape
babyLearning.hi: nanhe bhaav bante hue, chhoti bhaved aur honth aakaar lete hue

insight.en: Long before any school, you are your child's first teacher. Not through lessons, but simply through who you are.
insight.hi: Kisi bhi school se bahut pehle, aap apne bachche ki pehli teacher hain. Sabak se nahi, balki bas is se ki aap kaun hain.

expanded.en: We imagine teaching as something formal — lessons, rules, instructions. But your child's deepest learning begins long before any of that, by watching you. The way you speak when you are tired, how you treat a stranger, what you do when something goes wrong — these are the first lessons, taught without a single word. This is not a pressure to be perfect; it is an invitation to be honest. Your child will learn far more from how you live than from anything you tell them. You are already teaching, simply by being yourself with care.
expanded.hi: Hum sikhaane ko kuch formal samajhte hain — sabak, niyam, hidaayatein. Par aapke bachche ki sabse gehri seekh in sab se bahut pehle shuru hoti hai, aapko dekh kar. Jis tarah aap thake hue baat karti hain, kisi anjaan se kaise pesh aati hain, jab kuch galat ho jaaye to kya karti hain — yeh pehle sabak hain, ek bhi shabd ke bina sikhaaye. Yeh perfect hone ka dabaav nahi hai; yeh imaandaar hone ka nyota hai. Aapka bachcha jo aap kehti hain us se kahin zyada is se seekhega ki aap kaise jeeti hain. Aap pehle se hi sikhaa rahi hain, bas dhyan ke saath khud hone se.

deepDive.en: Developmental psychologists call this 'observational learning' — children absorb behaviour by watching trusted adults far more readily than by being told. The example you set is the strongest lesson you give.
deepDive.hi: Developmental psychologists ise 'observational learning' kehte hain — bachche bharose wale badon ko dekh kar vyavhaar utna jaldi seekhte hain jitna kehne se nahi. Aap jo misaal rakhti hain wahi sabse mazboot sabak hai jo aap deti hain.

remember.en: Your child learns who to be by watching who you are.
remember.hi: Aapka bachcha kaun banna hai yeh aapko dekh kar seekhta hai.

summary.en: A greedy dog learns that chasing more can cost us what we already hold.
summary.hi: Ek laalchi kutta seekhta hai ki zyada ke peeche bhaagna humse woh chheen leta hai jo hamare paas pehle se hai.

body.en:
A hungry dog wandered through the lanes until at last he found a fine, meaty bone. Delighted, he picked it up in his jaws and trotted off to enjoy it in peace. His path home crossed a narrow wooden bridge over a still, clear stream.

As he crossed, the dog looked down and saw another dog in the water below, carrying a bone just as fine as his own. He did not understand that he was looking at his own reflection. Greedy for more, he wanted that second bone too. He growled to frighten the other dog into dropping it — but the moment he opened his mouth, his own bone slipped out and fell with a splash into the stream.

The water rippled, the reflection vanished, and the bone sank from sight. The dog stood on the bridge with nothing at all. In wanting more than he had, he had lost the good thing already in his grasp.
body.hi:
Ek bhookha kutta galiyon mein ghoomta raha jab tak aakhir use ek badhiya, gosht bhari haddi na mil gayi. Khush hokar, usne use apne jabde mein uthaaya aur shaanti se khaane ke liye chal pada. Uska ghar ka raasta ek shaant, saaf nadi ke upar ek sankri lakdi ki pul se hokar guzarta tha.

Jaise hi woh paar hua, kutte ne neeche dekha aur paani mein ek doosra kutta dekha, jo uski apni jaisi hi badhiya haddi liye tha. Use samajh nahi aaya ki woh apna hi pratibimb dekh raha hai. Zyada ke laalach mein, use woh doosri haddi bhi chahiye thi. Usne doosre kutte ko daraane ke liye gurraaya taaki woh haddi chhod de — par jaise hi usne munh khola, uski apni haddi phisal kar ek chhapaak ke saath nadi mein girr gayi.

Paani mein leherein uthi, pratibimb gaayab ho gaya, aur haddi nazron se doob gayi. Kutta pul par bilkul khaali khada reh gaya. Jo uske paas tha us se zyada chaahne mein, usne woh achhi cheez kho di jo pehle se uski pakad mein thi.

title.en: Tell your baby a story from your grandparents' lives — a tale of where you all come from.
title.hi: Apne baby ko apne dada-dadi ya nana-nani ki zindagi se ek kahani sunaayein — ek kissa ki aap sab kahaan se aaye hain.

motivation.en: Your child belongs to a story that began long before them — let them hear its beginning.
motivation.hi: Aapka bachcha ek aisi kahani ka hissa hai jo unse bahut pehle shuru hui — unhe uski shuruaat sunne dein.

description.en: A short meditation to feel your bond grow
description.hi: Apne bond ko badhte mehsoos karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: With every quiet breath, the thread between us grows a little stronger.
affirmation.hi: Har shaant saans ke saath, hamare beech ka dhaaga thoda aur mazboot hota hai.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and rest both hands gently on your belly. Take three slow breaths and let your body settle. Imagine a soft, warm thread of light running from your heart down to your baby, glowing a little brighter with each breath you take.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur dono haath pyaar se apne pet par rakhein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur apne sharir ko thamne dein. Ek naram, garm roshni ka dhaaga sochein jo aapke dil se neeche aapke baby tak jaata hai, har saans ke saath thoda aur chamakta hua.

closingThought.en: That gentle thread is always there, even when life grows busy. You can return to it any time.
closingThought.hi: Woh naram dhaaga hamesha wahan hai, tab bhi jab zindagi vyast ho jaaye. Aap kabhi bhi uske paas laut sakti hain.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: Rest a hand low on your belly. Breathe in slowly and feel it rise under your hand, then let it fall as you breathe out. Five soft rounds, just you and your bump.
content.hi: Ek haath apne pet par neeche rakhein. Dheere saans andar lein aur use apne haath ke neeche uthte mehsoos karein, phir saans chhodte hue use girne dein. Paanch naram baar, bas aap aur aapka bump.

remember.en: Slow belly breaths calm you and your baby together.
remember.hi: Dheemi pet ki saansein aapko aur aapke baby ko saath shaant karti hain.

babyLearning.en: tiny ears settling into place, ready to listen soon
babyLearning.hi: nanhe kaan apni jagah baithte hue, jald sunne ko taiyaar

title.hi: Woh Nakal Karte Hain, Hukm Nahi Maante

insight.en: Children rarely do what we tell them. They do what they see us do.
insight.hi: Bachche shaayad hi woh karte hain jo hum kehte hain. Woh wahi karte hain jo woh humein karte dekhte hain.

expanded.en: Every parent learns this sooner or later: you can say 'be patient' a hundred times, but if you sigh and snap when you are rushed, that is the lesson that lands. Children are watching long before they are listening. They copy the tone of our voice, the way we handle a hard moment, how we speak about others when we think it does not matter. This is not a reason to feel watched and judged. It is a gentle freedom — you do not have to lecture. You simply have to live, as well as you can, the things you hope your child will one day carry.
expanded.hi: Har parent yeh jaldi ya der se seekhta hai: aap sau baar keh sakti hain 'sabra rakho,' par agar aap jaldi mein aah bhar kar bhadak jaati hain, to wahi sabak baithta hai. Bachche sunne se bahut pehle dekh rahe hote hain. Woh hamari aawaz ka lehza, mushkil pal ko sambhaalne ka tareeqa, aur jab humein lagta hai koi farq nahi padta tab doosron ke baare mein kaise bolte hain — sab nakal karte hain. Yeh dekhe aur parkhe jaane ka dar mehsoos karne ki wajah nahi hai. Yeh ek naram aazaadi hai — aapko bhaashan nahi dena hai. Aapko bas jeena hai, jitna achha kar sakein, woh cheezein jo aap chahti hain aapka bachcha ek din apnaaye.

deepDive.en: Studies of young children show imitation is one of their earliest and strongest learning tools — they mirror actions even before they grasp the words behind them. Modelling teaches faster than instruction.
deepDive.hi: Chhote bachchon par hui studies dikhaati hain ki nakal unke sabse pehle aur mazboot seekhne ke auzaaron mein se ek hai — woh shabd samajhne se pehle hi harkaton ki nakal karte hain. Misaal rakhna hidaayat se jaldi sikhaata hai.

remember.en: What you do speaks louder than what you say.
remember.hi: Aap jo karti hain woh us se zyada bolta hai jo aap kehti hain.

summary.en: A greedy jackal's appetite gets the better of his good sense.
summary.hi: Ek laalchi geedar ki bhookh uski achhi samajh par haavi ho jaati hai.

body.en:
A hungry jackal was prowling through a battlefield long after the fighting had ended, searching for any scrap to eat. Among the scattered weapons he found a hunter's bow, its tight string still in place. The jackal sniffed it and caught the faint smell of meat where the string was wrapped, for it was made of dried animal sinew.

Delighted, he began to gnaw eagerly at the bowstring. It tasted good, and he chewed faster and faster, thinking only of his hunger. But as he bit through the last strand, the bow snapped back with sudden force. The sharp wooden end struck deep, and the greedy jackal was harmed by the very feast he had been so pleased to find.

Had he paused and thought even once, he would have seen the danger. But hunger had made him careless, and he learned too late that rushing greedily into a thing can turn a small reward into a great regret.
body.hi:
Ek bhookha geedar ladaai khatam hone ke bahut baad ek rann-bhoomi mein ghoom raha tha, khaane ke kisi bhi tukde ki talaash mein. Bikhre hue hathiyaaron ke beech use ek shikaari ka dhanush mila, jiski kasi hui dori abhi bhi lagi thi. Geedar ne use soonghaa aur dori ke lipte hisse se gosht ki halki mehek paayi, kyunki woh sookhi pashu naso se bani thi.

Khush hokar, woh dori ko chaav se kutarne laga. Use swaad aaya, aur woh tezi se chabaata gaya, sirf apni bhookh ke baare mein sochte hue. Par jaise hi usne aakhri taar kaata, dhanush achaanak zor se peeche palta. Tej lakdi ka sira gehra lagaa, aur laalchi geedar usi daawat se ghaayal hua jise paakar woh itna khush tha.

Agar woh ek baar bhi ruk kar soch leta, to use khatra dikh jaata. Par bhookh ne use laaparwaah bana diya tha, aur usne der se seekha ki laalach mein kisi cheez mein jaldbaazi karna ek chhote inaam ko ek bade pachhtaawe mein badal sakta hai.

title.en: Tell your baby where your family name comes from, and the people who carried it before you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aapke parivar ka naam kahaan se aaya, aur woh log jinhone aapse pehle use thaama.

motivation.en: A name carries a history — let your baby feel the roots they are growing from.
motivation.hi: Ek naam apne saath ek itihaas leke chalta hai — apne baby ko un jadon ka ehsaas hone dein jinse woh badh raha hai.

description.en: Evening raga for warmth and gentle longing
description.hi: Shaam ka raag, garmjoshi aur naram chaah ke liye

affirmation.en: As this evening melody deepens, my heart softens warmly around you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh shaam ki dhun gehri hoti hai, mera dil garmjoshi se tumhare around naram hota hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: In this trimester, hunger can arrive suddenly and sharply. A small handful of nuts, a banana or a few soaked dates kept close by can steady that wave before it tips into a headache or a low mood.
content.hi: Is trimester mein, bhookh achaanak aur tez aa sakti hai. Paas rakhe mutthi bhar mewe, ek kela ya kuch bheege khajoor us lehar ko sambhaal sakte hain isse pehle ki woh sar dard ya kharab mood mein badal jaaye.

remember.en: Keeping a little snack near you is a small act of self-care.
remember.hi: Apne paas thoda snack rakhna khud ki dekhbhaal ka ek chhota tareeqa hai.

babyLearning.en: tiny fingers learning to curl and grip
babyLearning.hi: nanhi ungliyaan murna aur pakadna seekhti hui

title.hi: Aapki Aadatein Unki Ban Jaati Hain

insight.en: The small habits you repeat each day quietly become the ones your child grows up to keep.
insight.hi: Jo chhoti aadatein aap har din dohraati hain woh chupchaap wahi ban jaati hain jo aapka bachcha bada hokar apnaata hai.

expanded.en: Children do not inherit our grand intentions; they inherit our daily routines. How we greet the morning, whether we say thank you, how we speak about our own bodies, the way we rest or rush — these repeat in front of small eyes until they feel simply like 'how things are done.' This can sound like pressure, but it is really a quiet kind of hope. The gentle habits you tend to now — a kind word, a calm pause, a moment of gratitude — are seeds you are planting. You do not need new ones. You only need to keep the good ones warm.
expanded.hi: Bachche hamari badi neeyaton ko viraasat mein nahi paate; woh hamari rozana ki aadaton ko paate hain. Hum subah ka swagat kaise karte hain, dhanyavaad kehte hain ya nahi, apne sharir ke baare mein kaise baat karte hain, aaram karte hain ya jaldi mein rehte hain — yeh nanhi aankhon ke saamne dohraaye jaate hain jab tak woh bas 'aise hi hota hai' jaisa na lagne lagein. Yeh dabaav lag sakta hai, par yeh asal mein ek shaant ummeed hai. Jo naram aadatein aap abhi sambhaalti hain — ek dayaalu shabd, ek shaant pal, kritagyata ka ek lamha — woh beej hain jo aap bo rahi hain. Aapko nayi aadaton ki zaroorat nahi. Aapko bas achhi waali garm rakhni hain.

deepDive.en: Researchers find that family routines and everyday rituals shape a child's habits and sense of stability more than occasional big efforts. Consistency, not intensity, is what children absorb.
deepDive.hi: Researchers paate hain ki parivar ki dincharya aur rozana ke rituals ek bachche ki aadaton aur sthirta ke ehsaas ko kabhi-kabhaar ki badi koshishon se zyada gadhte hain. Sthirta, tezi nahi, wahi hai jo bachche apnaate hain.

remember.en: Your daily routines become your child's normal.
remember.hi: Aapki rozana ki aadatein aapke bachche ka aam ban jaati hain.

summary.en: A young boy's steady faith stays gentle and unshaken through every test.
summary.hi: Ek nanhe ladke ka sthir vishwaas har imtihaan mein naram aur achal bana rehta hai.

body.en:
Long ago there lived a young boy named Prahlad, the son of a powerful king. His father wished everyone to bow only to him, but gentle Prahlad believed in a kindness and goodness far greater than any throne. Quietly, without anger, the boy held to what he felt was true in his heart.

His father grew displeased and set hard tests before him, hoping to frighten the boy into changing. Through each one, Prahlad stayed calm and unafraid. He did not boast, he did not rage; he simply kept his faith soft and steady, trusting that goodness would not abandon him.

In the end, his quiet courage carried him safely through, and his unwavering kindness was honoured. Prahlad's story is remembered for a tender truth: that faith held gently, without fear or pride, has a strength of its own that even the mightiest cannot shake.
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle Prahlad naam ka ek nanha ladka rehta tha, ek shaktishaali raja ka beta. Uske pita chaahte the ki sab sirf unhe pranaam karein, par naram Prahlad ek aisi dayaalta aur achhaai mein vishwaas karta tha jo kisi bhi singhaasan se kahin badi thi. Chupchaap, bina gusse ke, ladke ne use thaame rakha jo use apne dil mein sach lagta tha.

Uske pita naaraz hue aur uske saamne kathin imtihaan rakhe, ladke ko daraa kar badalne ki ummeed mein. Har ek mein, Prahlad shaant aur nidar bana raha. Usne sheykhi nahi maari, woh nahi bharka; usne bas apna vishwaas naram aur sthir rakha, yeh bharosa karte hue ki achhaai use nahi chhodegi.

Aakhir mein, uski shaant himmat use surakshit paar le gayi, aur uski achal dayaalta ka samman hua. Prahlad ki kahani ek komal sachai ke liye yaad ki jaati hai: ki naram tarah se thaama gaya vishwaas, bina dar ya ghamand ke, apni ek taakat rakhta hai jise sabse shaktishaali bhi hila nahi sakte.

title.en: Tell your baby about a recipe passed down in your family — the dish that tastes like home.
title.hi: Apne baby ko apne parivar mein chali aa rahi ek recipe ke baare mein bataayein — woh pakwaan jo ghar jaisa lagta hai.

motivation.en: Some of our deepest memories live in a familiar taste — share one your child will inherit.
motivation.hi: Hamari kuch sabse gehri yaadein ek jaane-pehchaane swaad mein basti hain — ek aisi baant dein jo aapka bachcha viraasat mein paayega.

title.en: I Live The Goodness I Wish For You
title.hi: Main Woh Achhaai Jeeti Hoon Jo Tumhare Liye Chahti Hoon

affirmation.en: I do not need to teach goodness with words. I will simply live it, gently, so you grow up surrounded by it.
affirmation.hi: Mujhe achhaai shabdon se sikhaane ki zaroorat nahi. Main use bas jeeungi, narmi se, taaki tum usse ghire bade ho.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: I Lead By Living, Not Lecturing
title.hi: Main Jeekar Raah Dikhati Hoon, Bhaashan Dekar Nahi

content.en: I do not have to have all the right words. The way I live, gently and honestly, is the truest thing I can pass on.
content.hi: Mere paas saare sahi shabd hone ki zaroorat nahi. Jis tarah main jeeti hoon, narmi aur imaandaari se, wahi sabse sachi cheez hai jo main aage de sakti hoon.

remember.en: You teach most by simply being your gentle self.
remember.hi: Aap sabse zyada bas apna naram roop hokar sikhaati hain.

babyLearning.en: tiny muscles strengthening, little stretches beginning
babyLearning.hi: nanhi maanspeshiyaan mazboot hoti hui, chhoti angdaaiyaan shuru

insight.en: A child believes what they see far more than what they are told. Showing is the truest kind of teaching.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha jo dekhta hai us par us se kahin zyada vishwaas karta hai jo use bataaya jaata hai. Dikhaana sikhaane ka sabse sachcha tareeqa hai.

expanded.en: We can tell a child to be kind, to share, to stay calm — but words alone rarely take root. What truly teaches is letting them see kindness happen: watching you help a neighbour, share the last sweet, apologise when you are wrong. Children are quietly checking whether our actions match our words, and they trust the actions every time. This is wonderful news, because it means you do not need clever explanations. You only need to let your child witness, again and again, the small good things you already do. Showing turns a value from an idea into something real.
expanded.hi: Hum ek bachche ko keh sakte hain ki dayaalu bano, baanto, shaant raho — par akele shabd shaayad hi jad pakadte hain. Jo sach mein sikhaata hai woh hai unhe dayaalta hote dekhne dena: aapko padosi ki madad karte, aakhri mithaai baantte, galat hone par maafi maangte dekhna. Bachche chupchaap parakhte rehte hain ki hamari harkatein hamare shabdon se mel khaati hain ya nahi, aur woh har baar harkaton par bharosa karte hain. Yeh shaandaar khabar hai, kyunki iska matlab hai ki aapko chalaak samjhaane ki zaroorat nahi. Aapko bas apne bachche ko, baar-baar, woh chhoti achhi cheezein dekhne deni hain jo aap pehle se karti hain. Dikhaana ek value ko ek vichaar se kuch asli mein badal deta hai.

deepDive.en: Research on moral development shows children align their behaviour with what adults do, not just what they say — and notice the gap between the two keenly. Lived example is the most convincing lesson.
deepDive.hi: Naitik vikaas par hui research dikhaati hai ki bachche apna vyavhaar is se milaate hain ki bade kya karte hain, sirf kya kehte hain is se nahi — aur dono ke beech ke farq ko gaur se note karte hain. Jee kar dikhaayi gayi misaal sabse bharosemand sabak hai.

remember.en: Let your child see the values you hope they keep.
remember.hi: Apne bachche ko woh values dekhne dein jo aap chahti hain woh apnaaye.

summary.en: A gentle hare shows that the greatest gift is to give without holding back.
summary.hi: Ek naram khargosh dikhaata hai ki sabse bada tohfa hai bina kuch roke dena.

body.en:
In a quiet forest lived four friends — an otter, a jackal, a monkey and a hare. The little hare was known above all for his generous heart. One evening, a tired traveller came to the forest, weak with hunger and asking for food. The otter offered fish, the jackal brought scraps, and the monkey gathered ripe mangoes from the trees.

But the hare had only grass to eat, which would not nourish a hungry man. He thought hard about how he too could give something of worth. With a calm and loving heart, he decided to offer the only thing he truly had — himself. He gathered sticks for a small fire and gently leapt towards it, ready to give his very own body so the traveller would not go hungry.

Moved beyond words by such pure, selfless kindness, the traveller — who was no ordinary man — lifted the little hare to safety. To honour him forever, his gentle image was placed upon the moon, where it is said to shine to this day.
body.hi:
Ek shaant jungle mein chaar dost rehte the — ek oodbilaav, ek geedar, ek bandar aur ek khargosh. Nanha khargosh sabse zyada apne udaar dil ke liye jaana jaata tha. Ek shaam, ek thaka hua musafir jungle mein aaya, bhookh se kamzor, aur khaana maangne laga. Oodbilaav ne machhli di, geedar tukde laaya, aur bandar pedon se pake aam le aaya.

Par khargosh ke paas khaane ko sirf ghaas thi, jo ek bhookhe insaan ko poshan nahi deti. Usne gehraai se socha ki woh bhi kuch keemti kaise de sakta hai. Ek shaant aur pyaar bhare dil se, usne woh ek cheez dene ka faisla kiya jo sach mein uske paas thi — khud. Usne ek chhoti aag ke liye lakdiyaan jamaa ki aur naram tarah uski taraf kood gaya, apna khud ka sharir dene ko taiyaar taaki musafir bhookha na rahe.

Aisi shuddh, nishkaam dayaalta se shabdon se pare dravit hokar, musafir — jo koi aam insaan nahi tha — ne nanhe khargosh ko surakshit utha liya. Use hamesha ke liye samman dene ke liye, uski naram chhavi chaand par rakh di gayi, jahaan kaha jaata hai woh aaj tak chamakti hai.

title.en: Tell your baby about a place that matters to your family's history — a village, a home, a corner of the world.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aisi jagah ke baare mein bataayein jo aapke parivar ke itihaas mein maayne rakhti hai — ek gaon, ek ghar, duniya ka ek kona.

motivation.en: Knowing where they come from helps a child feel rooted in the world.
motivation.hi: Yeh jaanna ki woh kahaan se aaye hain ek bachche ko duniya mein jadon se juda mehsoos karaata hai.

description.en: Morning raga for a calm, settled start
description.hi: Subah ka raag, ek shaant, sthir shuruaat ke liye

affirmation.en: As this morning raga unfolds, a quiet steadiness settles in me, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh subah ka raag khulta hai, mujhmein ek shaant sthirta baithti hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: If the same meals start to feel dull, you do not need a big change — a squeeze of lemon, a handful of fresh herbs, or eating somewhere pleasant can make familiar food feel inviting again. Eating well is easier when it feels nice.
content.hi: Agar wahi khaana feeka lagne lage, to aapko bade badlaav ki zaroorat nahi — thoda nimbu nichod lein, mutthi bhar taazi herbs daalein, ya kisi achhi jagah baith kar khaayein, jaana-pehchaana khaana phir se acha lagne lagega. Achha khaana aasaan hota hai jab woh acha lage.

remember.en: Making a meal pleasant is part of nourishing yourself.
remember.hi: Khaane ko acha banana khud ko poshan dene ka hissa hai.

babyLearning.en: growing more responsive, sensing your gentle movements
babyLearning.hi: zyada responsive hote hue, aapki naram harkatein mehsoos karte hue

insight.en: Even when you think no lesson is happening, little eyes are quietly learning from you.
insight.hi: Tab bhi jab aapko lage koi sabak nahi chal raha, nanhi aankhein chupchaap aapse seekh rahi hoti hain.

expanded.en: The truest teaching often happens in the unguarded moments — not when we are trying to set an example, but when we forget we are being watched. How we react to spilt tea, the patience we show in a slow queue, the way we speak to ourselves when we make a mistake. Children gather all of it. This is not meant to make you anxious; no one is graceful every minute. It is simply a tender reminder that your ordinary, in-between moments are full of quiet lessons. Being a little gentler with yourself, even when no one seems to be looking, is teaching too.
expanded.hi: Sabse sachcha sikhaana aksar un palon mein hota hai jab hum sambhle nahi hote — tab nahi jab hum misaal rakhne ki koshish kar rahe hote, balki tab jab hum bhool jaate hain ki humein dekha ja raha hai. Hum giri hui chai par kaise react karte hain, ek dheemi line mein kitna sabra dikhaate hain, galti karne par khud se kaise baat karte hain. Bachche yeh sab samet lete hain. Yeh aapko chintit karne ke liye nahi hai; koi har minute graceful nahi rehta. Yeh bas ek komal yaad-dahaani hai ki aapke aam, beech ke pal shaant sabkon se bhare hain. Khud ke saath thoda aur naram hona, tab bhi jab koi dekh nahi raha lagta, yeh bhi sikhaana hai.

deepDive.en: Studies show children pick up on incidental, unplanned behaviour — including how adults treat themselves — as strongly as on deliberate teaching. The off-stage moments shape them too.
deepDive.hi: Studies dikhaati hain ki bachche anjaane, bina plan ke vyavhaar ko — is samet ki bade khud se kaise pesh aate hain — utni hi mazbooti se samajhte hain jitna soche-samjhe sikhaane ko. Bina-stage waale pal bhi unhe gadhte hain.

remember.en: Be gentle with yourself — small eyes learn that too.
remember.hi: Khud ke saath naram rahein — nanhi aankhein woh bhi seekhti hain.

title.en: Tenali Rama And The Brinjal Curry
title.hi: Tenali Rama Aur Baingan Ki Sabzi

summary.en: Witty Tenali Rama turns his own words back on himself with a clever lesson.
summary.hi: Hazirjawaab Tenali Rama apne hi shabd ek chalaak seekh ke saath khud par mod leta hai.

body.en:
One day the royal cook prepared a brinjal curry so delicious that the whole court praised it. Tenali Rama, delighted, declared loudly, 'This brinjal is the finest vegetable in all the land! There is nothing better!' The king, amused, ordered that brinjal curry be served at every meal from then on.

Day after day the brinjal curry appeared, until even Tenali Rama grew thoroughly tired of it. At last he grumbled, 'This brinjal is dreadful — who could eat such a thing every day?' The king raised an eyebrow. 'But Tenali, only last week you called it the finest vegetable in the land!'

Tenali Rama bowed with a twinkle in his eye. 'Your Majesty, I serve you, not the brinjal. The brinjal has never paid me a single coin, so why should I defend it?' The whole court burst into laughter, and the king, laughing hardest of all, gladly ordered a change of menu.
body.hi:
Ek din shaahi baawarchi ne baingan ki sabzi itni laziz banaayi ki poore darbaar ne uski taareef ki. Tenali Rama, khush hokar, zor se bola, 'Yeh baingan poore desh ki sabse badhiya sabzi hai! Is se behtar kuch nahi!' Raja ne, mazaa lete hue, hukm diya ki ab se har khaane mein baingan ki sabzi parosi jaaye.

Din par din baingan ki sabzi aati rahi, jab tak khud Tenali Rama bhi usse poori tarah oob na gaya. Aakhir woh bhunbhunaaya, 'Yeh baingan to bekaar hai — bhala ise har din kaun khaa sakta hai?' Raja ne bhauh uthayi. 'Par Tenali, pichhle hi hafte tumne ise desh ki sabse badhiya sabzi kaha tha!'

Tenali Rama aankhon mein chamak liye jhuka. 'Maharaj, main aapki seva karta hoon, baingan ki nahi. Baingan ne mujhe ek bhi sikka nahi diya, to main uski tarafdaari kyun karoon?' Poora darbaar hansi se goonj utha, aur raja, sabse zyada hansta hua, khushi-khushi menu badalne ka hukm de diya.

title.en: Tell your baby about a family value you are proud of — something you hope they will carry too.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aise family value ke baare mein bataayein jis par aapko garv hai — kuch jo aap chahti hain woh bhi apnaaye.

motivation.en: The values you name today become the compass your child will steer by.
motivation.hi: Jo values aap aaj bataati hain woh woh disha-soochak ban jaate hain jisse aapka bachcha raah chunega.

title.hi: Mere Shaant Pal Bhi Sikhaate Hain

affirmation.en: I will be as kind to myself as I hope you will be to yourself. My gentleness is a lesson you are already learning.
affirmation.hi: Main khud ke saath utni hi dayaalu rahungi jitni main chahti hoon tum apne saath raho. Meri narmi ek sabak hai jo tum pehle se seekh rahe ho.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Main Khud Se Pyaar Se Baat Karti Hoon

content.en: The voice I use with myself is the voice my child will one day learn. I will make it a gentle one.
content.hi: Jo aawaz main khud ke saath istemaal karti hoon wahi aawaz mera bachcha ek din seekhega. Main use ek naram aawaz banaungi.

remember.en: Self-kindness is a lesson your child overhears.
remember.hi: Khud par dayaalta ek sabak hai jo aapka bachcha sunta hai.

babyLearning.en: growing softly, a fine downy layer forming
babyLearning.hi: naram tarah badhte hue, ek mahin roeyenwali parat banti hui

title.hi: Values Pakdi Jaati Hain, Sikhaayi Nahi Jaati

insight.en: A child does not memorise honesty or kindness. They catch it, like warmth, from the people around them.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha imaandaari ya dayaalta yaad nahi karta. Woh use, garmjoshi ki tarah, apne aas-paas ke logon se pakadta hai.

expanded.en: We cannot hand a child our values the way we hand them a book. Honesty, generosity, courage — these are not facts to be learned but qualities that seep in slowly, caught from the air of a loving home. A child raised among gentleness grows gentle; one surrounded by gratitude grows grateful. This means the most powerful thing you can do is not to explain your values, but to live among them yourself, letting your child breathe them in daily. You are creating the very atmosphere your child's character will form in — and that atmosphere is made of who you choose to be.
expanded.hi: Hum ek bachche ko apni values waise nahi de sakte jaise hum unhe ek kitaab dete hain. Imaandaari, udaarta, himmat — yeh seekhne ke tathya nahi balki gun hain jo dheere-dheere andar samaate hain, ek pyaar bhare ghar ki hawa se pakde jaate hain. Narmi ke beech paala gaya bachcha naram badhta hai; kritagyata se ghira hua kritagya badhta hai. Iska matlab hai ki sabse shaktishaali cheez jo aap kar sakti hain woh apni values samjhaana nahi, balki unke beech khud jeena hai, apne bachche ko unhe rozana saans lene dena. Aap wahi mahaul bana rahi hain jismein aapke bachche ka charitra banega — aur woh mahaul is se banta hai ki aap kaun banna chunti hain.

deepDive.en: Researchers describe values as 'transmitted through modelling and atmosphere' rather than direct instruction — children adopt the moral climate they grow up in. The home's everyday tone shapes character most.
deepDive.hi: Researchers values ko seedhi hidaayat ke bajaaye 'modelling aur mahaul ke zariye pohanchaayi gayi' bataate hain — bachche us naitik mahaul ko apnaate hain jismein woh bade hote hain. Ghar ka rozana ka lehza charitra ko sabse zyada gadhta hai.

remember.en: Children breathe in the values they grow up among.
remember.hi: Bachche un values ko saans mein lete hain jinke beech woh bade hote hain.

summary.en: An elephant never forgets the small kindness of a stranger who helped him.
summary.hi: Ek haathi us anjaan ki chhoti dayaalta kabhi nahi bhoolta jisne uski madad ki.

body.en:
Deep in a forest, a great elephant trod on a sharp thorn that drove painfully into his foot. He could barely walk, and he limped about in misery for days, unable to remove it himself. The other animals were too frightened of his size to come near and help.

Then a poor woodcutter passing through saw the suffering elephant. Though afraid, his kind heart would not let him walk away. Gently, he approached, soothed the great creature with soft words, and carefully drew the thorn from its foot. The elephant trumpeted with relief and looked at the man with grateful eyes before lumbering away into the trees.

Years later, the woodcutter was in danger, lost and weary far from home. Out of the forest came an elephant who knelt before him, lifted him onto its back, and carried him safely to his village. It was the very same elephant, who had never once forgotten the kindness shown to him. A good turn, freely given, is rarely forgotten.
body.hi:
Ek jungle mein gehre andar, ek vishaal haathi ke pair mein ek tej kaanta chubh gaya jo dard se andar dhans gaya. Woh muskil se chal paata, aur kai din takleef mein langdaata raha, use khud nahi nikaal paaya. Doosre jaanwar uske aakaar se itne dare hue the ki paas aakar madad na kar sake.

Tabhi wahaan se guzarta ek gareeb lakadhara dukhi haathi ko dekha. Darne ke baawajood, uske dayaalu dil ne use chal dene nahi diya. Naram tarah, woh paas aaya, vishaal jeev ko naram shabdon se shaant kiya, aur dhyan se uske pair se kaanta nikaal diya. Haathi ne raahat se chinghaada aur aadmi ko kritagya aankhon se dekha, phir pedon ke beech chala gaya.

Saalon baad, lakadhara khatre mein tha, ghar se door bhatka aur thaka hua. Jungle se ek haathi nikla jo uske saamne jhuk gaya, use apni peeth par uthaaya, aur surakshit uske gaon tak le gaya. Yeh wahi haathi tha, jo us par ki gayi dayaalta ko ek baar bhi nahi bhoola tha. Bina shart di gayi ek achhi madad shaayad hi bhulaayi jaati hai.

title.en: Tell your baby about an elder or ancestor you admire — someone whose spirit you hope lives on in them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aise bujurg ya purvaj ke baare mein bataayein jiski aap kadar karti hain — koi jiski rooh aap chahti hain unmein zinda rahe.

motivation.en: The ones we admire live on in the stories we keep telling about them.
motivation.hi: Jinki hum kadar karte hain woh un kahaaniyon mein zinda rehte hain jo hum unke baare mein sunaate rehte hain.

description.en: Night raga for tenderness and rest
description.hi: Raat ka raag, komalta aur aaram ke liye

affirmation.en: As this night melody softens the dark, a tender calm wraps around us both.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh raat ki dhun andhere ko naram karti hai, ek komal shaanti hum dono ko lapet leti hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: If meals sit heavily by evening, a lighter supper eaten a little earlier can help you rest more comfortably. Warm, simple foods — a bowl of khichdi, soup, or daliya — are kind to a tired stomach at the end of the day.
content.hi: Agar shaam tak khaana bhaari lage, to thoda jaldi khaaya gaya halka raat ka khaana aapko zyada aaram se aaram karne mein madad kar sakta hai. Garm, saadi cheezein — ek katori khichdi, soup, ya daliya — din ke ant mein thake pet par dayaalu hoti hain.

remember.en: A light, warm evening meal helps your body settle into rest.
remember.hi: Ek halka, garm shaam ka khaana aapke sharir ko aaram mein baithne mein madad karta hai.

babyLearning.en: closing the week, growing rounder and stronger
babyLearning.hi: hafta poora karte hue, aur gol aur mazboot hote hue

title.en: Be What You Hope To See
title.hi: Wahi Banein Jo Aap Dekhna Chahti Hain

insight.en: The simplest way to raise the child you dream of is to become, gently, the person you hope they grow into.
insight.hi: Jis bachche ka aap sapna dekhti hain use paalne ka sabse saada tareeqa hai narmi se woh insaan ban jaana jismein aap chahti hain woh bade.

expanded.en: All week we have seen the same quiet truth from different angles: children learn most from who we are. So the kindest goal is not to perfect your child, but to keep becoming the kind of person you hope they will be. If you wish them to be patient, practise patience; if you hope they will be kind, let kindness be your habit. You will never do this flawlessly, and you do not need to. You only need to keep facing in the right direction. Be what you hope to see — and your child, watching closely, will slowly grow towards it too.
expanded.hi: Poore hafte humne wahi shaant sachai alag-alag pehluon se dekhi: bachche sabse zyada is se seekhte hain ki hum kaun hain. To sabse dayaalu lakshya apne bachche ko perfect banana nahi hai, balki wahi tarah ka insaan bante rehna hai jaisa aap chahti hain woh banein. Agar aap chahti hain woh sabra rakhein, sabra ka abhyaas karein; agar aap chahti hain woh dayaalu banein, dayaalta ko apni aadat banayein. Aap yeh kabhi bina galti ke nahi karengi, aur aapko zaroorat bhi nahi. Aapko bas sahi disha mein munh kiye rehna hai. Wahi banein jo aap dekhna chahti hain — aur aapka bachcha, gaur se dekhte hue, dheere-dheere uski taraf badhega bhi.

deepDive.en: Long-term studies find that parents' own consistent behaviour predicts children's values better than rewards or rules. Becoming the example is the most lasting form of guidance.
deepDive.hi: Lambe samay ki studies paati hain ki parents ka apna sthir vyavhaar bachchon ki values ko inaam ya niyamon se behtar bataata hai. Misaal ban jaana margdarshan ka sabse sthayi roop hai.

remember.en: Become the person you hope your child becomes.
remember.hi: Wahi insaan banein jo aap chahti hain aapka bachcha bane.

summary.en: The leaves of a tree learn that their beauty is fed by the humble roots below.
summary.hi: Ek ped ki pattiyaan seekhti hain ki unki khoobsurati neeche ki vinamra jadon se palti hai.

body.en:
A tall tree spread its bright green leaves proudly in the sunlight, swaying for all to admire. The leaves felt very grand. 'How beautiful we are,' they whispered to one another, 'dancing high in the breeze, drinking the warm sun. Surely we are the finest part of this tree.'

Deep below the soil, the roots heard them but said nothing. They simply went on with their quiet, unseen work — drawing up water, holding the great tree firm against the wind, feeding every leaf above.

Then came a long, dry season. The roots searched deeper and deeper through the hard earth to find what little water remained, and patiently sent it up to keep the leaves alive. Only then did the leaves understand. 'We shine in the light,' they said softly, 'but it is the roots, hidden and humble, who give us all our life.' What we admire on the surface is so often held up by what works quietly, unseen, beneath.
body.hi:
Ek ooncha ped dhoop mein apni chamakdaar hari pattiyaan garv se phailaaye, sabki taareef ke liye lehraata tha. Pattiyon ko bahut shaan mehsoos hoti. 'Hum kitni khoobsurat hain,' woh ek doosre se phusphusaati, 'hawa mein ooper naachti, garm dhoop peeti. Zaroor hum is ped ka sabse badhiya hissa hain.'

Mitti ke gehre neeche, jadon ne unhe suna par kuch nahi kaha. Woh bas apna shaant, andekha kaam karti rahi — paani kheenchti, bade ped ko hawa ke khilaaf mazbooti se thaamti, ooper ki har patti ko poshan deti.

Phir ek lamba, sookha mausam aaya. Jadein kathin zameen mein aur gehre dhoondti gayi taaki jo thoda paani bacha ho woh mil jaaye, aur sabra se use ooper bhejti rahi taaki pattiyaan zinda rahein. Tabhi pattiyon ko samajh aaya. 'Hum roshni mein chamakti hain,' unhone dheere se kaha, 'par yeh jadein hain, chhipi aur vinamra, jo humein hamari saari zindagi deti hain.' Jiski hum oopar taareef karte hain woh aksar usse thama hota hai jo chupchaap, andekha, neeche kaam karta hai.

title.en: Tell your baby how your family marks its milestones — the birthdays, festivals and moments you gather for.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aapka parivar apne khaas palon ko kaise manaata hai — woh janmdin, tyohaar aur lamhe jinke liye aap sab jamaa hote hain.

motivation.en: The way a family celebrates becomes the warm memory a child carries for life.
motivation.hi: Jis tarah ek parivar khushiyaan manaata hai woh garm yaad ban jaati hai jo ek bachcha zindagi bhar saath rakhta hai.

description.en: A short meditation to rest in your own steadiness
description.hi: Apni khud ki sthirta mein aaram karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I do not have to become someone new for you. I only have to settle, gently, into the best of who I already am.
affirmation.hi: Mujhe tumhare liye koi naya insaan nahi banna. Mujhe bas, narmi se, jo main pehle se hoon uske sabse achhe roop mein thamna hai.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your shoulders drop. Take three slow breaths and feel yourself settle, like water growing still. Bring to mind one quality you love in yourself — your warmth, your patience, your care — and let it grow a little brighter inside you, knowing your baby rests within it.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur apne kandhon ko girne dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur khud ko thamta mehsoos karein, jaise paani shaant hota hai. Apne aap mein ek gun yaad karein jise aap pyaar karti hain — aapki garmjoshi, aapka sabra, aapki dekhbhaal — aur use apne andar thoda aur chamakne dein, yeh jaante hue ki aapka baby usi ke andar aaram kar raha hai.

closingThought.en: The best of who you are is already enough to grow a beautiful child around. Carry that quiet trust with you.
closingThought.hi: Jo aapka sabse achha roop hai woh pehle se hi kaafi hai ek sundar bachche ko uske around paalne ke liye. Us shaant bharose ko apne saath le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: Breathe in slowly through your nose, pause for a gentle moment, then breathe out a little longer than you breathed in. Just three of these, and notice how your whole body settles.
content.hi: Apni naak se dheere saans andar lein, ek naram pal rukein, phir saans andar lene se thodi lambi saans baahar chhodein. Aisi sirf teen, aur dekhein kaise aapka poora sharir tham jaata hai.

remember.en: A longer breath out is the body's own way back to calm.
remember.hi: Ek lambi saans baahar chhodna sharir ka khud ka shaanti mein lautne ka tareeqa hai.
